_comment: Registered output example — LUT output captured by flip-flop
_explanation: This demonstrates using flip-flops for sequential (clocked) logic.
LUT A is configured as an inverter (NOT gate using I0 only):
When I0=0 → output 1 (index 0), when I0=1 → output 0 (index 1)
The flip-flop captures the LUT output on each clock rising edge.
By feeding the FF output back to the LUT input, you get a toggle
This is the building block of all counters and state machines.
